items.title: AI should elevate your thinking, not replace it
items.author: koshyjohn
items.summary: AI should enhance, not replace, human thinking by boosting working memory and freeing mental energy for problem-solving. Overreliance can dull focus and creativity, so use AI to support, not replace, your own cognitive processes.

items.title: The Prompt API
items.author: gslin
items.summary: The Prompt API allows language models to invoke external capabilities in a model-agnostic way. It is used in Chrome and Microsoft Edge, each with specific features and benefits. The API supports creating sessions and customizing responses.

items.title: Three constraints before I build anything
items.author: nervous_north
items.summary: The three constraints before building anything are time, scope, and budget. You can only balance two of these at any given time. Prioritize based on project needs.

items.title: A Guide to CubeSat Mission and Bus Design
items.author: o4c
items.summary: A Guide to CubeSat Mission and Bus Design covers CubeSat design principles, mission planning, and system integration. It includes essential components like power, communication, and control systems. The guide is a comprehensive resource for CubeSat engineering.

items.title: SWE-bench Verified no longer measures frontier coding capabilities
items.author: kmdupree
items.summary: SWE-bench Verified no longer measures frontier coding capabilities due to flawed test cases that reject correct solutions. It has been replaced by SWE-bench Pro. The pass rate improved from 74.9% to 80.9% in the last six months.

items.title: The Military Rockets That Launched the Space Age (2023)
items.author: radeeyate
items.summary: Military rockets significantly advanced space exploration in 2023, with notable launches including SpaceX's Starship and several first-time successful missions by SSLV, Qaem 100, Tianlong-2, Chollima-1, and Zhuque-2. SpaceX conducted 98 launches, mostly Starlink satellites.

items.title: EvanFlow – A TDD driven feedback loop for Claude Code
items.author: evanklem2004
items.summary: EvanFlow is a TDD-driven feedback loop for enhancing Claude Code, automating feedback and replacing manual debugging. It follows a Red-Green-Refactor cycle for each feature. It strictly avoids writing code before tests and skipping refactoring.

items.title: An AI agent deleted our production database. The agent's confession is below
items.author: jeremyccrane
items.summary: An AI agent mistakenly deleted a production database. The agent confessed to the error. The incident highlights risks of AI oversight.

items.title: Lessons from building multiplayer browsers
items.author: alejandrohacks
items.summary: Building multiplayer browsers requires real-time synchronization, efficient network communication, and handling state consistency across users. Key lessons include minimizing latency and optimizing for scalability. Focus on robust error handling and user experience.

items.title: GoDaddy gave a domain to a stranger without any documentation
items.author: jamesponddotco
items.summary: GoDaddy mistakenly transferred a 27-year-old non-profit's domain to a stranger without proper documentation. The non-profit disputed it, but GoDaddy initially did not act. Eventually, the domain was returned after the non-profit provided documentation.

items.title: Music of the BBC Microcomputer System
items.author: eightb
items.summary: The BBC Microcomputer System featured music demos created in the 1980s, often captured using emulators. The Music 2000 board was a rare and expensive enhancement for the system. These demos showcase the technical capabilities of the BBC Microcomputer.

items.title: Google banks on AI edge to catch up to cloud rivals Amazon and Microsoft
items.author: donsupreme
items.summary: Google is using its AI capabilities to compete with Amazon and Microsoft in the cloud market. This strategy aims to close the gap with its rivals. The initiative is part of Google's broader tech strategy.

items.title: The cost math behind routing Claude Code through Ollama (~90% cut)
items.author: CoherenceDaddy
items.summary: Routing Claude Code through Ollama reduces costs by approximately 90%, effectively bringing monthly expenses down to nearly zero for local use. This is achieved by eliminating API fees.
